# Implementation Plan: Interpretation mode & neutral band

**Branch**: `903-card-ui-composition` | **Date**: 2026-04-21 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)  
**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/903-card-ui-composition/spec.md`

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit.plan`. Phase 0 ‚Üí [research.md](./research.md). Phase 1 ‚Üí [data-model.md](./data-model.md), [contracts/](./contracts/), [quickstart.md](./quickstart.md).

## Summary

Add **`interpretation`** (`consumption` | `production`, default consumption) and optional **`neutral_interpretation`** (percent threshold `T`, default **2**) so the **comparison narrative**, **interpretation icon**, and **chart delta** semantic colors align with user intent (consumption vs production) and a **neutral band** when **`|p| ‚â§ T`** (`p` = same signed % as the delta chip). When the chip is in a **placeholder** state (no reliable `p`), use a **separate insufficient-data** path (**FR-903-W**): dedicated `localize()` strings and muted/non-judgment styling, distinct from neutral-band ‚Äúsimilar.‚Äù Forecast | Total copy is **unchanged** by `interpretation`. Release **1.1.0** with tests, **CHANGELOG**, README / README-advanced, wiki per **907**.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.6+ (strict), ES modules  
**Primary Dependencies**: Lit 3, Apache ECharts 5.x, Luxon 3.x, Home Assistant Lovelace APIs (`hass`, `hui-*` / `ha-*` elements)  
**Storage**: N/A (in-browser; config via Lovelace YAML / UI)  
**Testing**: Vitest (`npm test`, `TZ=UTC vitest run`)  
**Target Platform**: Modern browsers running Home Assistant frontend (HACS-distributed card bundle)  
**Project Type**: Home Assistant custom Lovelace card (single `src/` package, Vite build)  
**Performance Goals**: No additional blocking work on main thread; semantic evaluation O(1) per render  
**Constraints**: HA theme CSS variables for colors; no new npm deps for YAML parsing in card (use HA `jsyaml` in editor only); user-visible strings via `localize()` only  
**Scale/Scope**: Two new YAML keys + editor field for `interpretation` only; `neutral_interpretation` YAML-only in v1 per spec

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-checked after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Assessment |
|-----------|--------------|
| **I. HA / HACS** | PASS ‚Äî extends existing `CardConfig`; editor uses established `ha-form` + shallow-merge; semver + changelog planned. |
| **II. Security / resilience** | PASS ‚Äî case-insensitive enum parse, numeric bounds for `T`, fallbacks (consumption, `T=2`); invalid `interpretation` does not brick card; user strings via i18n JSON. |
| **III. TypeScript / tests / docs** | PASS ‚Äî strict types for config + semantic outcome; Vitest unit tests for mapping; README/wiki/changelog for 1.1.0. |
| **IV. UX / theming / a11y** | PASS ‚Äî semantic states use HA tokens; insufficient-data path avoids misleading ‚Äúsimilar‚Äù; ARIA/labels follow existing card patterns. |
| **V. Performance / simplicity** | PASS ‚Äî single shared semantic helper; no new network or heavy deps. |

**Post-design**: No new violations; design stays within one card bundle and existing modules.

## Implementation phases (execution order)

1. **Types & config parsing**: Extend `CardConfig` with `interpretation`, `neutral_interpretation`; defaults and validation per **FR-903-P**, **FR-903-V**.  
2. **`computeInterpretationSemantics`** (name TBD): Inputs: current, reference, chip percent `p` availability, `interpretation`, `T`. Outputs: `SemanticOutcome` = `positive` \| `negative` \| `neutral` \| `insufficient_data` + trend hint. Unit-test all branches including reference=0 and placeholder chip.  
3. **Narrative row**: Bind `localize()` keys for consumption / production / neutral band / **insufficient data**; icons + HA token colors per **FR-903-R** / **FR-903-W**.  
4. **Chart**: Pass semantic outcome (or parallel color role) into **902** renderer so delta segment matches narrative.  
5. **Editor**: Add `interpretation` to schema; preserve `neutral_interpretation` via YAML merge (**904**).  
6. **i18n**: `en` / `pl` / `de` for all new keys.  
7. **907**: CHANGELOG **1.1.0**, README, README-advanced, wiki.
